claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. To do this properly, I'll need to gather some information about your circumstances and any children you're responsible for.

Let me start with my first question:

**How many children are you responsible for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm responsible for one child - my son Alex, who is 17 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 17 years old, there are some specific rules that apply.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training (for example, at school, college, or an apprenticeship)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he's still in college doing his A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. That's helpful information.

Now, I need to ask about your living situation:

**Do you live with Alex, or does he live with someone else?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, thank you. Now I need to check a few more details about your circumstances to ensure you meet all the eligibility requirements.

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Now I have a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Do you have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, pre-settled status, or are you a British citizen/have indefinite leave to remain in the UK?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that confirmation. Now I just need to check one more important detail:

**Is anyone else claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or are you the only person who could be claiming for him?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him - just me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, I now have all the information I need to assess your eligibility. Let me review the rules carefully:

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 17 years old and in approved education (A-levels at college)
- You live with Alex in the UK
- You're a British citizen
- You're the only person claiming for him
- You're responsible for his care

According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. However, for children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training.

Since Alex is 17 and currently in approved education (A-levels), you **do qualify** for Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Your son meets all the requirements:
- He's 17 years old and under 20
- He's in approved education (A-levels)
- He lives with you in the UK
- You're a British citizen
- You're the only person claiming for him

If you haven't already done so, you can make a claim for Child Benefit. Is there anything else you'd like to know?

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and in approved education (A-levels at college). According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex meets this criterion and lives with the claimant in the UK, and the claimant is a British citizen with no one else claiming for Alex, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 17-year-old son Alex, who is in approved education (A-levels) and lives with them in the UK. The claimant is a British citizen and is the sole person claiming for this child, meeting all eligibility criteria for a 16-19 year old in approved education or training."

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 17 years old and in approved education (A-levels at college). According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex meets this criterion and lives with the claimant in the UK, and the claimant is a British citizen with no one else claiming for Alex, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 17-year-old son Alex, who is in approved education (A-levels) and lives with them in the UK. The claimant is a British citizen and is the sole person claiming for this child, meeting all eligibility criteria for a 16-19 year old in approved education or training."
